As Directors deeply committed to advancing our Company’s sustainability vision, we are pleased to present this year’s Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report—an affirmation of our continued dedication to Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) principles.

Building on the foundation laid in previous years, we have made noteworthy progress in embedding sustainability across all facets of our operations. Through innovation, strategic collaboration, and a forward-looking approach, we have effectively tackled ESG challenges.

This year, we are proud to report further initiatives in waste generation, renewable energy initiatives, and meaningful community engagement programs. These milestones not only underscore our persistent efforts but also reflect our long-term vision of responsible and inclusive growth.

Our ESG journey is far from over. With renewed energy and a clear road map, we remain committed to pushing boundaries, setting new benchmarks, and driving sustainable transformation within our industry.

The entity has formulated a comprehensive Anti-Corruption and Anti-Bribery Policy, which outlines our zero-tolerance approach towards any form of corruption, bribery, or unethical business practices. The policy has been internally reviewed and approved by the management and is currently in place as part of our internal governance framework.

To further strengthen its formal adoption, the policy is scheduled for approval by the Board in the upcoming Board Meeting to be held in the month of May. Upon Board approval, the policy will be made publicly available and widely communicated across all levels of the organization to ensure strict compliance and awareness.
No such action was taken or is underway for the year under review.

The entity has well-established processes in place to proactively identify, manage, and avoid conflicts of interest involving members of the Board. These processes are guided by our Code of Conduct and Board Governance policies, which mandate full disclosure of any potential conflicts and require Directors to recuse themselves from discussions or decisions where such conflicts may arise.

All Board members are required to submit periodic declarations regarding their interests, and any potential conflict is reviewed by the Board or its relevant committees to ensure transparency, accountability, and adherence to the highest standards of corporate governance. This framework reinforces our commitment to ethical leadership and responsible decision-making.

Rishabh complies with the Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) obligations under the Plastic Waste Management Rules. The Company has partnered with CPCB-authorized plastic waste recyclers to collect and recycle plastic waste on its behalf. This ensures the environmentally sound recycling of the target quantity assigned to Rishabh.
Rishabh has implemented a structured system for the collection and safe disposal of end-of-life Electrical and Electronic Equipment (EEE) in collaboration with an authorized e-waste aggregator.

Hazardous waste generated at the manufacturing facilities is securely managed and disposed of through authorized agencies and recyclers approved by the respective State Pollution Control Boards (SPCBs), in accordance with applicable regulations.

the company has implemented an Occupational Health and Safety Management System aligning with required standards, effectively encompassing all operations across the entire manufacturing plants.
Risk mitigation processes are implemented wherever work-related hazards or potential risks are identified. Appropriate control measures are established and documented.

The company regularly conducts training sessions for employees covering various aspects to ensure a safe and healthy work place & culture such as Electrical and safety standards, 5S Practices, Housekeeping, First aid, Fire safety  and evacuation drills to promote awareness of company policies and emergency response procedures.

Key stakeholder groups are identified based on their relevance, influence, and interest in the company’s operations and long-term success. Key stakeholders include employees, customers, suppliers, investors, regulatory authorities, and the local communities where we operate.

We prioritize regular, transparent, and meaningful engagement with these stakeholders through structured communication channels, feedback systems, and collaborative initiatives. This approach enables us to foster inclusivity, build trust, and support informed and responsible decision-making across all levels of our business.

Stakeholder consultation is integral to our ESG strategy and decision-making process. We actively engage with key stakeholders—including customers, vendors, employees, and regulatory bodies—to identify and manage material environmental and social topics relevant to our operations.

For instance, several of our customers and vendors have specifically requested detailed information related to ESG compliance and performance initiatives, which they included in their own sustainability disclosures and due diligence processes. In response, we provided structured data and narrative inputs to support these requirements, ensuring transparency and alignment with their ESG expectations.

These engagements have led us to adopt more structured ESG reporting initiative practices internally, strengthen our supply chain due diligence processes, and enhance our environmental and social performance metrics. Feedback from such consultations has also informed the development of our sustainability policies/procedures employee welfare programs, and waste management initiatives.

Shareholders and investor communities are regularly informed about the company’s performance, strategic developments, and key material events on a quarterly basis. This includes updates on the outcomes of Board and committee meetings to ensure transparency and support informed investment decisions.

The Board of Directors meets at regular intervals to review and oversee the company’s overall business performance. These meetings focus on evaluating financial results, operational efficiency, strategic initiatives, risk management practices, and compliance with regulatory requirements. The Board provides strategic guidance, assesses progress against organizational goals, and makes critical decisions to drive sustainable growth and long-term value creation for all stakeholders.

KMPs lead strategic operations meetings to align long-term goals, optimize resources, and improve efficiency. They also drive business performance meetings to assess financial health, operational success, and market growth. Together, these meetings ensure the company stays competitive and agile, with strategy and performance constantly aligned.

Meetings for permanent employees focus on personal well being and operational business needs. Well being meetings address health, work-life balance, engagement, and professional development, ensuring employees are supported and valued. Operational meetings focus on workforce planning, training, safety, and efficiency to meet business objectives. Together, they foster a motivated, productive workforce and a smooth-running organization.

Customer Meetings: Product Information and Features

Meetings with customer are designed to provide detailed information about products and their features, ensuring clear communication and fostering trust. These meetings serve as a platform to:

Present Product Details: Highlight key features, functionalities, and benefits, ensuring consumers fully understand the product.

Address Customer Needs: Identify and address specific consumer requirements, offering tailored solutions and product recommendations.

Gather Feedback: Collect insights on product performance and customer satisfaction to drive improvements.
These discussions strengthen Customer  relationships, inform purchasing decisions, and promote transparency, ensuring that the products align with Customer  expectations.

Supply chain meetings are conducted to ensure all operational requirements are effectively managed and aligned with business goals. These meetings focus on streamlining procurement, inventory control, and logistics to support smooth and timely operations. Key discussions include demand forecasting, vendor coordination, and material availability to meet production needs. They also address operational challenges, supply disruptions, and risk mitigation strategies. Emphasis is placed on cost-effectiveness, resource optimization, and compliance. By aligning supply chain functions with business demands, these meetings help maintain continuity, efficiency, and responsiveness across the value chain.

Local community meetings focus on promoting community welfare and sharing information about specific activities or initiatives. They aim to address local needs related to health, education, and sustainability, while encouraging participation, feedback, and collaboration. These meetings foster trust, transparency, and stronger relationships between the organization and the community.

The Company has established a structured internal mechanism for addressing grievances related to human rights. Employees may report complaints to any member of the designated Grievance Redressal Committee. Upon receipt, such complaints are shared with all committee members and formally addressed. However, employees are free to submit grievances in any clear and coherent manner.

Minor issues are handled promptly by the HR & Administration team. Major or serious grievances submitted in writing are duly investigated by the Committee in an impartial manner. If the grievance is found valid, HR initiates corrective actions. In cases where the complaint is found to be baseless or motivated by personal interest, it is dismissed with appropriate feedback or warning to the complainant.
Company has standard policy &  Internal Complaints Committee to address such issues. The care of confidentiality maintenance is taken by HR

From a production standpoint, there is negligible water requirement as the major manufacturing processes are non-water-intensive. Water consumption is limited to domestic usage, primarily for sanitation and hygiene purposes. The wastewater generated is routed to an on-site Sewage Treatment Plant (STP), where it undergoes treatment and is subsequently recycled for non-portable applications such as landscape gardening and toilet flushing.

Company is committed to environmentally responsible waste disposal in line with applicable regulations. We have established partnerships with Extended Producer Responsibility  (EPR) for the safe and compliant management of e-waste, and with EPR for the recycling and handling of plastic waste, supporting sustainable practices and extended producer responsibility.
In addition, for the disposal of hazardous materials, we have appointed an agency authorized by the state board.

Yes, the entity has robust Business Continuity and Disaster Management Plans in place, which have been developed and implemented internally by the management. These plans are designed to ensure operational resilience, safeguard critical assets, and enable timely response and recovery in the event of unforeseen disruptions. The framework covers key risk scenarios, crisis communication protocols, and continuity strategies across business functions. While currently managed at the internal level, the plans are periodically reviewed and tested to ensure effectiveness. The entity is committed to formalizing and integrating these plans further into its broader governance structure in due course.

Rishabh Instruments Limited has established a formal grievance redressal mechanism to uphold ethical standards and ensure transparent communication with both internal and external stakeholders, including local communities.
A dedicated reporting system is in place through emails, feedback forms, and direct interactions, enabling stakeholders to raise concerns related to ethics, compliance, fraud, misconduct, financial irregularities, human rights, workplace behavior, discrimination, harassment, safety violations, and environmental issues.
The mechanism allows employees, suppliers, and community members to report grievances confidentially. All reports are reviewed by a designated team and addressed through a structured process that ensures fair investigation and timely resolution, in line with the Company’s Code of Conduct and applicable regulations.

Customer complaints and feedback are received through various channels, including email, phone calls, and product returns, typically communicated via Sales Engineers or Service personnel. Upon receipt, each case is carefully reviewed to assess the nature and cause of the issue. A thorough investigation is conducted, involving relevant departments if necessary, to ensure accurate resolution. Based on the findings, appropriate corrective or preventive actions are identified and implemented. 

Once the review is complete, a clear and timely response is provided to the customer, ensuring transparency and reinforcing our commitment to continuous improvement and customer satisfaction.
